name: i-adjectives and na-adjectives
summary: Japanese has two adjective types: i-adjectives end in い and conjugate, while na-adjectives behave like nouns and take な.
explanation:
Japanese adjectives come in two families. I-adjectives end in the kana い, like 大きい (ookii, big) and 高い (takai, expensive/tall). They conjugate by themselves: the past of 高い is 高かった (takakatta, was expensive), and the negative is 高くない (takakunai, not expensive). When they describe a noun directly, they attach with no extra word: 大きい家 (ookii ie, a big house).

Na-adjectives, like 静か (shizuka, quiet) and 便利 (benri, convenient), behave more like nouns. To describe a noun, they take な: 静かな部屋 (shizukana heya, a quiet room). At the end of a sentence they use です like a noun: この部屋は静かです (kono heya wa shizuka desu). Their past and negative use でした and ではありません, just like nouns.

The key is to know which family an adjective belongs to. A reliable test: if it ends in い and that い is part of the word's natural form, it is usually an i-adjective. A few words like きれい (kirei, pretty) end in い but are actually na-adjectives, so they are worth memorising as exceptions.

conjugation.i-adj present: 高いです (takai desu) - is expensive
conjugation.i-adj negative: 高くないです (takakunai desu) - is not expensive
conjugation.i-adj past: 高かったです (takakatta desu) - was expensive
conjugation.na-adj present: 静かです (shizuka desu) - is quiet
conjugation.na-adj before noun: 静かな部屋 (shizukana heya) - a quiet room
conjugation.na-adj negative: 静かではありません (shizuka dewa arimasen) - is not quiet

examples.native: この料理は美味しいです。(kono ryouri wa oishii desu.)
大きい犬がいます。(ookii inu ga imasu.)
昨日は寒かったです。(kinou wa samukatta desu.)
この町は静かです。(kono machi wa shizuka desu.)
便利な店を見つけました。(benrina mise o mitsukemashita.)
彼女はきれいな人です。(kanojo wa kireina hito desu.)

commonMistakes: Adding な to an i-adjective. 大きいな is wrong; i-adjectives attach directly (大きい家).
Conjugating na-adjectives like i-adjectives. The past of 静か is 静かでした, not 静かかった.
Forgetting that きれい and 有名 (yuumei) are na-adjectives despite their endings.
Forming the i-adjective negative as 高いくない. Drop the い first: 高くない.
Using です after the past い-adjective incorrectly. 高かったです is right; 高いでした is wrong.
tip: First decide the family. I-adjectives conjugate themselves; na-adjectives lean on な and です like nouns.
